_comment: Stryker.NET config TEMPLATE — copy to <project-root>/stryker-config.json and customize. Parallel JSON variant of stryker.config.template.mjs (TS) — Stryker.NET uses JSON not JS config syntax.
_usage: 2. Replace {{TODO}} placeholders with your project values
4. Override default Category=Unit filter via run-mutation.ts flags: --include-integration or --include-e2e
_prerequisites: Test project MUST have green initial test run (no external infra deps in default scope)
If integration tests require live DB/auth/services — they are SKIPPED by default via Category=Unit filter
To make tests Stryker-ready: mark them with [Trait("Category", "Unit")] for pure unit tests

stryker-config._test-case-filter_comment: DEFAULT skip Integration/E2E. Override via run-mutation.ts --include-integration / --include-e2e flags which append to filter.
stryker-config._additional-timeout_comment: Per-mutant test timeout (NOT timeout-ms — that's invalid key in Stryker.NET).
stryker-config._concurrency_comment: Tune based on CPU. 4 is safe baseline; bump to half of cores. Don't max out — host vitest workers + test process pool compete.
stryker-config._mutation-level_comment: Options: Basic / Standard / Advanced / Complete. Standard = good baseline coverage. Advanced/Complete для critical paths.

thresholds._comment: Empirical defaults from OutSight AI case study. Critical paths ≥70%, standard ≥50%, experimental ≥30%.

stryker-config._env_passthrough_comment: Stryker.NET inherits process env. Set repo-specific vars BEFORE dotnet-stryker — e.g., ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Test, CI=true. Stryker config 'env' field is UNSUPPORTED — must be in shell.
